project.name: blitzed-out
project.description: Adult party board game (local, online multiplayer, solo, shared-device) built with React 19 + TypeScript + Vite, MUI v9, Zustand, Dexie/IndexedDB, Firebase, i18next, and simple-peer WebRTC video.

nodes.name: cleanupVideoCallSignaling
nodes.summary: Scheduled Firebase Function running every 5 minutes that removes empty WebRTC video call rooms and purges stale offers, answers, and ICE candidates older than 2 minutes.

nodes.name: manualCleanupAnonymousAccounts

nodes.name: useSchedule.ts
nodes.summary: Thin hook exporting the ScheduleContext value for consuming schedule state within the provider tree.

nodes.name: useUserList.ts
nodes.summary: Thin hook exporting the UserListContext value for consuming online user list state within the provider tree.

nodes.name: messages.tsx
nodes.summary: React context provider that subscribes to Firebase room messages, hydrates the Zustand messages store, and exposes message state to descendants.

nodes.name: MessagesProvider
nodes.summary: Reads the current room from router params, clears and reloads messages via Firebase on room change, and provides the message list to child components.

nodes.name: migration.tsx
nodes.summary: Complex migration context provider that coordinates IndexedDB language data migration, health checking, integrity verification, and recovery across language changes using lazy-loaded migration services.

nodes.name: MigrationProvider
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full migration lifecycle including status checking, integrity repair, language-change triggered re-migration, and recovery from failed migrations.

nodes.name: minimalAuth.tsx
nodes.summary: Lightweight auth presence provider that checks localStorage, sessionStorage, and cookies for an existing Firebase session without loading the full Firebase SDK.

nodes.name: MinimalAuthProvider
nodes.summary: Provides a lightweight initial auth state by inspecting browser storage, avoiding full Firebase initialization until the app fully loads.

nodes.name: schedule.tsx
nodes.summary: React context provider for game schedule state, wrapping Firebase schedule CRUD operations and syncing with the Zustand schedule store.

nodes.name: ScheduleProvider
nodes.summary: Manages schedule subscription, add/update/delete operations via Firebase, and exposes a memoized context value with CRUD actions to descendants.

nodes.name: theme.tsx
nodes.summary: Theme context provider and hooks for accessing and toggling dark/light/system theme mode, syncing with settings store and applying CSS classes to the document body.

nodes.name: ThemeProvider
nodes.summary: Reads theme mode from settings store and system media query, builds the MUI theme, and applies light/dark body CSS classes on mode change.

nodes.name: userList.tsx
nodes.summary: React context provider that subscribes to the Firebase room presence list, validates incoming user objects, and syncs online users to the Zustand user list store.

nodes.name: UserListProvider
nodes.summary: Subscribes to Firebase presence on room change, batches user updates, validates entries with isValidOnlineUser, and exposes the user list to descendants.

nodes.name: cssUrl.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the cssUrl helper, verifying URL sanitization and CSS url() string construction across various input cases.

nodes.name: cssUrl.ts
nodes.summary: Exports a single helper that sanitizes a URL string for safe use inside a CSS url() value, escaping special characters and non-ASCII code points.

nodes.name: cssUrl
nodes.summary: Sanitizes a URL string by escaping backslashes, special characters, and high code-point characters so it is safe for use in CSS url() declarations.

nodes.name: momentLocale.ts
nodes.summary: Provides dayjs locale helpers (and backward-compat moment aliases) for mapping i18n language codes to locale-aware date instances used across the app.
nodes.languageNotes: Exports both new dayjs-based functions and legacy moment-named aliases to support a gradual migration from moment.js to dayjs.

nodes.name: getDayjsLocale
nodes.summary: Maps a supported i18n language code string to the corresponding dayjs locale string.

nodes.name: getDayjsWithLocale
nodes.summary: Returns a dayjs instance for the given date configured with the locale matching the provided language code.

nodes.name: statsFormatting.ts
nodes.summary: Exports pure formatting and computation helpers for game statistics: duration formatting, distribution analysis, average roll, and average game time calculations.

nodes.name: formatDuration
nodes.summary: Converts a millisecond duration into a human-readable string showing hours, minutes, and seconds.

nodes.name: timestamp.ts
nodes.summary: Exports a single robust parsing function that normalises Firebase Timestamp objects, numeric epoch values, ISO strings, and Date objects into a JavaScript Date.

nodes.name: parseMessageTimestamp
nodes.summary: Handles four distinct timestamp formats (Firebase Timestamp, numeric epoch, ISO string, Date) and returns a valid Date or null, with console warnings for unrecognised types.

nodes.name: useBrokenActionsState.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the useBrokenActionsState hook, verifying detection and state management of broken or invalid custom action tiles across migration states.

nodes.name: useFullscreenStatus.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite for useFullscreenStatus hook covering fullscreen entry, exit, browser vendor prefixes, and DOM event listener attachment/cleanup.

nodes.name: useHybridPlayerList.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Tests for the useHybridPlayerList hook that merges local and online player lists, covering authenticated vs. anonymous states and online/offline game modes.

nodes.name: useLocalPlayers.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Tests for the useLocalPlayers hook validating local player creation, update, removal, and persistence behaviour.

nodes.name: usePlayerMove.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Tests for the usePlayerMove hook covering dice rolling, position advancement, Firebase writes, and game-over detection for both local and online modes.

nodes.name: useRoomNavigate.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Tests for the useRoomNavigate hook verifying navigation logic between public and private rooms with proper URL construction and routing context.

nodes.name: useSettingsToFormData.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Minimal tests for the useSettingsToFormData hook ensuring settings store values are correctly mapped to form data shape.

nodes.name: useUnifiedActionList.test.tsx
nodes.summary: Tests for the useUnifiedActionList hook verifying merging of custom groups and tiles, deduplication, and final sorted action list construction.

nodes.name: useAnalytics.ts
nodes.summary: Provides the useAnalytics hook and a withAnalytics HOC that expose memoized tracking callbacks for feature usage, intensity selection, user flows, and game sessions.

nodes.name: useAnalytics
nodes.summary: Returns a set of memoized analytics tracking callbacks covering feature usage, intensity selection, user flows, feature discovery, and game session events.

nodes.name: withAnalytics
nodes.summary: Higher-order component that wraps a React component with analytics tracking, optionally firing a mount event via the useAnalytics hook.

nodes.name: useAuth.ts
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper hook that reads the AuthContext and throws a descriptive error when consumed outside the AuthProvider.

nodes.name: useAuth
nodes.summary: Consumes the AuthContext and throws a descriptive error if used outside the AuthProvider, returning the typed auth context value.

nodes.name: useBreakpoint.ts
nodes.summary: Single-function hook that wraps Material-UI useTheme and useMediaQuery to return a boolean indicating whether the viewport is below the specified breakpoint.

nodes.name: useBrokenActionsState.ts
nodes.summary: Hook that identifies broken or stale custom action tiles by comparing the current actions list against migration state, returning counts and affected tile identifiers.

nodes.name: useBrokenActionsState
nodes.summary: Computes the set of broken action tiles (tiles referencing missing action keys) using memoisation and integration with the migration context.

nodes.name: useCardSound.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the useCardSound hook, verifying that sound playback behavior respects settings store volume configuration.

nodes.name: useCardSound.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a React hook that wires up card-flip sound effects using the settings store volume to control playback.

nodes.name: useCardSound
nodes.summary: Hook that reads volume from the settings store and returns a sound player for card flip audio.

nodes.name: useCountdown.ts
nodes.summary: Implements a countdown timer hook with pause/resume support and an onComplete callback, using setInterval internally.

nodes.name: useCountdown
nodes.summary: Countdown timer hook that counts down from a starting number of seconds, supports pause/resume, and calls onComplete when the countdown reaches zero.

nodes.name: useDeferredMigration.ts
nodes.summary: Wraps the migration context to provide deferred, idempotent language migration triggering using microtask scheduling to avoid React render-phase side effects.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses queueMicrotask to defer migration triggering out of the render phase, preventing React strict-mode double-trigger issues.

nodes.name: useDeferredMigration
nodes.summary: Hook providing deferred language migration with options for automatic triggering on mount and manual trigger/ensure methods, guarded by a hasTriggered flag to ensure idempotency.

nodes.name: useFullscreenStatus.ts
nodes.summary: Provides cross-browser fullscreen management including enter/exit actions and real-time fullscreen state tracking via DOM events.
nodes.languageNotes: Handles vendor-prefixed fullscreen APIs (webkit, moz, ms) for broad browser compatibility.

nodes.name: useFullscreenStatus
nodes.summary: Hook returning fullscreen state and toggle function, listening for fullscreen change events and handling browser prefix differences.

nodes.name: useGroupData.ts
nodes.summary: Manages CRUD state for custom action groups, handling group loading, tile count aggregation, deletion with confirmation dialogs, and state reloading.

nodes.name: useGroupData
nodes.summary: Hook providing group management operations including loading groups with tile counts, delete confirmation flow, and group reload with effect-triggered initialization.

nodes.name: useGroupForm.ts
nodes.summary: Manages form state for creating and editing custom action groups, including intensity labels, template selection, validation, and submission logic.

nodes.name: useGroupForm
nodes.summary: Hook managing all group form state including label, type, intensity labels, template index, and editing group, with reset/populate callbacks and submit-time validation.

nodes.name: useGroupValidation.ts
nodes.summary: Provides memoized validation for custom group form fields, checking label presence, type selection, and duplicate intensity label names.

nodes.name: useGroupValidation
nodes.summary: Hook returning memoized validation errors for group form fields, checking that label is set, type is selected, and intensity labels are unique.

nodes.name: useHybridPlayerList.ts
nodes.summary: Merges local and remote player data into a unified player list, with type guard helpers for distinguishing local from remote players.

nodes.name: useHybridPlayerList
nodes.summary: Hook that combines local player store data with Firebase remote player list into a unified, sorted player array with consistent shape.

nodes.name: useLocalPlayers.ts
nodes.summary: Provides local (offline) player session management operations including session init/load/clear, player navigation, and position lookup via the local player store.

nodes.name: useLocalPlayers
nodes.summary: Hook exposing callbacks for all local player session operations: init, load, next player, clear, update settings, and position lookup by id or index.

nodes.name: usePlayerStats.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a reactive hook to fetch and return player statistics from Dexie for the currently authenticated user.

nodes.name: usePlayerStats
nodes.summary: Hook using useLiveQuery to reactively fetch player stats for the authenticated user from local IndexedDB storage.

nodes.name: useReturnToStart.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a callback hook that sends a Firebase message to reset the current player's position back to the start of the board.

nodes.name: useReturnToStart
nodes.summary: Hook returning a memoized callback that sends a return-to-start action message for the authenticated user via Firebase.

nodes.name: useRoomNavigate.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a navigation helper hook that normalizes room names and routes to the appropriate room URL using React Router.

nodes.name: useRoomNavigate
nodes.summary: Hook that normalizes a room name to uppercase and navigates to its route, reading the current room param from the URL.

nodes.name: useSoundAndDialog.ts
nodes.summary: Manages audio feedback and dialog display for incoming game action messages, handling dice sounds, TTS speech, message sounds, and haptic vibration.

nodes.name: useStatsTracking.ts

nodes.name: useStatsTracking
nodes.summary: Hook returning callbacks for trackTileLanding, trackGameComplete, and trackGameStart that silently catch and ignore stats recording errors.

nodes.name: useTTS.ts
nodes.summary: React hook wrapping the TTS (text-to-speech) service, managing playback state (isPlaying, isLoading, error) and exposing speak/stop callbacks with automatic cleanup on unmount.

nodes.name: useTurnIndicator.ts

nodes.name: useUrlImport.ts

nodes.name: useWakeLock.ts

nodes.name: useWakeLock

tour.title: Project Overview & Architecture
tour.description: Start here to understand what Blitzed Out is: an adult party board game built on React 19, TypeScript, Vite, MUI, Zustand, Dexie, and Firebase. The README names the project, the architecture doc lays out the full stack and the ports-and-adapters layering, and CONTEXT.md is the authoritative domain glossary (rooms, game modes, topology, roles, anatomy, casting) whose vocabulary recurs throughout the tour.

tour.title: Application Boot & Routing
tour.description: App.tsx is the runtime entry point: it bootstraps authentication via a minimal auth hook and conditionally renders either the full app or a loading skeleton. RouterSetup defines the React Router tree, lazy-loading the Room and Cast views and normalizing room-id casing. Together they answer 'how does the app start and decide what to render?'
tour.languageLesson: React Router lazy-loads route components via React.lazy + Suspense, so each view (Room, Cast) ships as a separate code-split chunk that downloads only when its route is visited.

tour.title: The Type Foundation
tour.description: Before any feature makes sense, learn the vocabulary the whole codebase shares. Settings.ts is the single most depended-upon module (highest fan-in) and describes game mode, board, player, and UI configuration; types/index.ts is the central barrel re-exporting custom-group, player, and validation types plus runtime constants. Nearly every later step consumes these shapes.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript barrel files (index.ts) re-export many modules behind one import path, giving the codebase a clean public surface and centralizing the domain's type vocabulary.

tour.title: State: Zustand Stores & Dexie
tour.description: State lives in two tiers. The Zustand settings store persists user settings, tracks analytics on changes, and exposes selector hooks built on the Settings type from Step 3. The Dexie BlitzedOutDatabase class defines the versioned IndexedDB schema (with v1/v2 migrations) backing every offline-capable table. This is the persistence backbone the rest of the app reads and writes through.
tour.languageLesson: Zustand stores are hooks created with create(); components subscribe to narrow selector slices so a change to one field re-renders only the components that read it.

tour.title: Configuring a Game: Settings & Board Build
tour.description: The GameSettings dialog orchestrates the app, board, and room settings tabs and triggers form submission; submitForm builds the room-settings message, updates the user profile, and dispatches via Firebase. The payoff is buildGame, the core generator that turns selected groups, intensities, and roles into a randomized tile sequence (shuffle bag plus start/finish tiles) — the actual game board.

tour.title: Playing a Room: Board, Tiles & Tokens
tour.description: The Room view is the live gameplay surface, wiring presence, player moves, turn transitions, wake lock, URL import, and the board/message/video panels. GameBoard renders the grid generated in Step 5, places player tokens, and animates token movement between tiles when player locations change. This is where the configured game becomes an interactive board.

tour.title: Taking a Turn: Dice & Players
tour.description: RollButton handles dice-roll computation, the countdown timer, haptics, and an imperative trigger ref for keyboard play, while DiceRoller renders the preloaded 3D dice animation toward the target value. Player position and turn order come from the hybrid player-list hook, which merges local and remote players so solo and multiplayer rooms share one model.

tour.title: Custom Content: Groups & Tiles
tour.description: Beyond the default board, players author their own content. The custom-groups store is the full Dexie CRUD/query layer for action groups (with dedup, availability mapping, and migration guards); the custom-tiles store offers filtered, paginated tile access with per-group counts. These records feed buildGame from Step 5, letting users extend the game without code changes.

tour.title: Localization & The Replacement Pipeline
tour.description: Tile text is data, not hardcoded strings. actionStringReplacement transforms a raw tile action into final display text by resolving role, player-name, and gender/role-aware anatomy placeholders; anatomyPlaceholderService maps tokens like {genital}, {hole}, and {chest} to locale-appropriate terms via i18next. The pre-bundled per-locale action files (e.g. English local bundle) are what the app actually loads at runtime.
tour.languageLesson: Game content uses placeholder tokens stored in canonical English; localized aliases are normalized on save and rendered back per-locale at display time, keeping the replacement pipeline locale-agnostic.

tour.title: Sync, Offline & Firebase
tour.description: The central Firebase service exposes auth, presence, messaging, image upload, board storage, and a caching/debounce layer over Firestore and RTDB. The sync orchestrator coordinates a full Firebase-to-local pull by delegating to entity-specific sync classes (settings, custom groups, custom tiles, game boards) and aggregating results — this is what keeps the Dexie tables from Steps 4 and 8 in step across devices.

tour.title: Backend Rules & Indexes
tour.description: The Firebase config wires Firestore rules/indexes, Realtime Database rules, Cloud Functions, and Hosting headers together. firestore.rules enforces authenticated access to the documents the sync engine from Step 10 reads and writes, while the composite indexes make its room/user/session queries efficient. This is the security and performance boundary behind the client-side sync.
tour.languageLesson: Firestore security rules run server-side on every read/write; composite indexes must be declared explicitly for any query that filters or orders on multiple fields, otherwise the query is rejected at runtime.

tour.title: Real-Time Video & Casting
tour.description: The capstone features layer onto an active room. VideoCall initializes a WebRTC session for the room on mount and tears it down on unmount, using the ICE/WebRTC settings in the config module. The Cast view is the receiver surface that renders live game state on Chromecast/AirPlay displays, handling anonymous login and autoplaying background media — turning a single device's room into a shared-screen experience.
